title: Urostachya djaratensis
intro: Urostachya djaratensis, commonly known as the 'forest fern', is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Borneo. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its numerous health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Urostachya djaratensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea, extract, or dried powder, and is used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Urostachya djaratensis is classified within the family Stemonuraceae and the order Celastrales. This species belongs to the genus Urostachya, which includes a limited number of species. The classification of Urostachya djaratensis is further supported by its distinct characteristics and molecular phylogenetic analysis. The family Stemonuraceae is a small group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical regions. As a member of the Celastrales order, Urostachya djaratensis shares similarities with other plants in this group.
